title: Understanding Rotational Motion
text: To find the acceleration of a point on a rotating object, we need to consider the centripetal acceleration formula.

title: Centripetal Acceleration Formula
text: The formula for centripetal acceleration is given by $$a=r {\omega}^2$$, where $$r$$ is the radius and omega is the angular velocity.

title: Convert Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity omega in radians per second can be found by converting the given revolutions per minute $$\frac{rev}{min}$$ to radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Convert $$360.0$$ $$\frac{rev}{min}$$ to $$\frac{rad}{s}$$. Use the conversion factor: $$1$$ rev $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ rad and $$1$$ min $$=$$ $$60$$ s.

title: Calculate Centripetal Acceleration
text: Use the formula $$a=r {\omega}^2$$ with $$r=0.1$$ $$m$$ and $$omega=37.7$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ to find the acceleration.
